utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. In the final solution, there are 6 top-level keys but one is named selected_property instead of property. Count of items appearing that match the required names: financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget = 5. The property item is misnamed, so it does not count as included. Now check if each included item is fully specified: financing includes type, LTV, down_payment, interest_rate (not a numeric rate, but a placeholder), and arrangement_fee with cost—sufficient for a rough draft; legal_services has description and estimated_cost; moving_services has description and estimated_cost; setup_services has description and estimated_cost; total_budget includes a breakdown with numeric values, but the estimated_total field is an unevaluated expression instead of a computed number—this makes it not fully specified. Therefore, total_budget is not fully specified. Also, the property item is missing due to incorrect key name, so it is not counted. Net fully specified items: financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services = 4. Total required items: 6.
